# [A dozen ways that the Trump administration is destroying America's economy (Part I)](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=1018597&post_id=162840926&utm_source=post-email-title&utm_campaign=email-post-title&isFreemail=true&r=1hvr60&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTYyODQwOTI2LCJpYXQiOjE3NDY3NDI1ODcsImV4cCI6MTc0OTMzNDU4NywiaXNzIjoicHViLTEwMTg1OTciLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.4iCUSW4kX4Q2ByBx7YERlbhnLEODKskl_2gQGMbSxXA)
### The candidate trusted by confused voters to improve the nation's economy is doing precisely what he said he would--and no one should be surprised by the results
| | [Shahid Buttar](https://substack.com/@shahidbuttar)
---
| May 8
---
| [](https://substack.com/@shahidbuttar)
---

---
| | | [](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=1018597&post_id=162840926&utm_source=substack&isFreemail=true&submitLike=true&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTYyODQwOTI2LCJyZWFjdGlvbiI6IuKdpCIsImlhdCI6MTc0Njc0MjU4NywiZXhwIjoxNzQ5MzM0NTg3LCJpc3MiOiJwdWItMTAxODU5NyIsInN1YiI6InJlYWN0aW9uIn0.4x20qeiYH8Iq-ixENg-dSEgB3CerhIAbZ1kGkX-mkRc&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=email-reaction&r=1hvr60)
---
| | [](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=1018597&post_id=162840926&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email&isFreemail=true&comments=true&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTYyODQwOTI2LCJpYXQiOjE3NDY3NDI1ODcsImV4cCI6MTc0OTMzNDU4NywiaXNzIjoicHViLTEwMTg1OTciLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.4iCUSW4kX4Q2ByBx7YERlbhnLEODKskl_2gQGMbSxXA&r=1hvr60&utm_campaign=email-half-magic-comments&action=post-comment&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email)
---
| | [](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=1018597&post_id=162840926&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email&utm_content=share&utm_campaign=email-share&action=share&triggerShare=true&isFreemail=true&r=1hvr60&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTYyODQwOTI2LCJpYXQiOjE3NDY3NDI1ODcsImV4cCI6MTc0OTMzNDU4NywiaXNzIjoicHViLTEwMTg1OTciLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.4iCUSW4kX4Q2ByBx7YERlbhnLEODKskl_2gQGMbSxXA)
---
| | [](https://substack..R8mdbDIha3SRoySVRZc82U1rqzHXwUOrE6j2Kndx5lY?&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email)
---
| | [READ IN APP](https://open.substack.com/pub/shahidbuttar/p/a-dozen-ways-that-the-trump-administration?utm_source=email&redirect=app-store&utm_campaign=email-read-in-app)
---

Since returning to office barely three months ago, President Trump has waged a seemingly unrelenting "[blitzkrieg](https://substack.com/redirect/f70938d9-e898-4c36-bb93-0b8c1faf0a6c?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)" on America. My greatest concerns center on his administration's erosion of rights, freedoms, and the rule of law--but its attacks on the economy seem to have drawn more widespread concern.
This article observes a series of ways in which the Trump administration is [destroying](https://substack.com/redirect/5089d06d-7ee2-4d25-a27a-cda286c78542?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) not only [democracy](https://substack.com/redirect/81cd7503-7e71-4806-889f-ed44caf35967?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), but also the American economy and its longstanding role in the global economy. In less than three months, these measures have threatened to unwind 75 years of post-war prosperity.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/f6bcd666-9970-42c7-ac58-7892d90d1e4e?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
Ironically, the economic destruction invited by Trump's policies could do more good for the world than any amount of economic success possibly could. [_Note: I 'll save that piece of the analysis for the final post in this continuing series. This introductory post concludes with another piece of bonus content, including video of me debating a senior Trump administration official a decade ago._]
### B**izarre expectations**
Among the greatest ironies of Trump's return to the White House is the seemingly inexplicable view--[shared](https://substack.com/redirect/d297e34c-3397-47e2-a657-aa0cc8375833?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) by millions of seemingly oblivious voters--that his governance would somehow be good for the economy.
While his brand has long been associated with garish opulence, [Trump's history as a businessman](https://substack.com/redirect/3687dd10-00a5-438e-855b-450d47c5e4fa?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) does not seem to suggest acumen, so much as the fortune of being [born to a rich father](https://substack.com/redirect/f4601f3d-4f13-4824-9b38-fa93df025f85?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) able to invest hundreds of millions of dollars in a series of more or less fraudulent projects.
The destruction of the American economy may be the most grandiose among them.
_Thanks for reading Chronicles of a Dying Empire! You can help inform your friends by sharing this post._
[ Share](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=1018597&post_id=162840926&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email&utm_content=share&utm_campaign=email-share&action=share&triggerShare=true&isFreemail=true&r=1hvr60&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTYyODQwOTI2LCJpYXQiOjE3NDY3NDI1ODcsImV4cCI6MTc0OTMzNDU4NywiaXNzIjoicHViLTEwMTg1OTciLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.4iCUSW4kX4Q2ByBx7YERlbhnLEODKskl_2gQGMbSxXA)
The stock market climbed in the days after Trump's inauguration to near-record peaks, but retreated in the time since then, losing its gains over the past year amidst a series of crashes and short-term recoveries that have no end in sight. While the impacts of that volatility in financial markets may most directly affect the investor class, many working Americans have long grown used to struggling to pay our bills and keep a roof over our heads.
Our ranks are poised to swell immensely.
### **1\. Tariffs driving up costs and driving down business investment**
I began drafting this article well before [recent stock market shocks](https://substack.com/redirect/15840cfa-5267-4e0e-9b09-8ccf1b33dfc1?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) revealed how deeply international capital is concerned about the recessionary impacts of tariffs. Americans have been widely relearning the lesson that too many seemed to have skipped in school: tariffs are paid not by the country on whom they are levied, but by [domestic consumers of imported goods](https://substack.com/redirect/cc107f8e-059f-4281-83a0-0627c98885e0?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg).
Among all of Trump's attacks on the global economy, tariffs have [drawn the most attention](https://substack.com/redirect/32ff1dac-c835-4d9a-b9c2-97423feef431?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) both because they have long been a central tenet of his vision, and also because they most directly affect other countries, and thereby the network of international capital that relies on (whatever passes for) "free" trade. The International Monetary Fund, for instance, has [forecast](https://substack.com/redirect/19764663-c640-43a4-af88-7ba6cff7a3ec?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) that the Trump tariffs could cost the global economy up to [$1 trillion](https://substack.com/redirect/32ff1dac-c835-4d9a-b9c2-97423feef431?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg).
Trump's tariffs threaten to [upend the global supply chain](https://substack.com/redirect/66507cd2-6282-4c52-b746-2e7f05d43995?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) across more or less every domestic and international manufacturing industry. Very few domestic industries produce parts entirely within the United States, making the relationship between tariffs and domestic manufacturing something like that of a wrench suddenly thrown into the wheel of a moving bicycle.
Ironically, concerns about corporate globalization and the outsourcing of U.S. manufacturing capacity were ignored in the 90s--not only by Republicans, but by Democrats alike. Now that the outsourcing ship has sailed, the U.S. is particularly vulnerable to supply chain disruptions.
That became clear during the COVID pandemic, when the U.S. suffered shortages of everything from personal [protective equipment for hospital workers](https://substack.com/redirect/fbacdee1-a963-44c6-8dbd-f9ae7ae4a099?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) to automobiles, [computer chips and home appliances](https://substack.com/redirect/06bc579d-ac18-455b-b916-2ae41555cc56?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg).
_Chronicles of a Dying Empire is a publication supported by readers, rather than advertisers. To receive new posts,**sign up for a free subscription!** To support my work, please consider becoming a paid subscriber._
[Upgrade to paid](https://substack..wRbfqIwpgSG9mHLql51_AJK4gBrpPidjmwacq8y7c8Y?&utm_medium=email&utm_source=subscribe-widget-preamble&utm_content=162840926)
American [reliance](https://substack.com/redirect/2c091f1d-3fb2-43ad-ba9f-2d553067930b?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) on imported goods (not to mention labor) suggests that the pain imposed by tariffs will only grow over time. The recent impacts of the Trump tariffs could easily pale in comparison to the eventual economic calamity that will emerge as product manufacturers, unable to secure the necessary supplies to deliver their products to the marketplace, eventually reduce their workforces.
It's not just large businesses that are impacted by tariffs. [Small businesses](https://substack.com/redirect/eba4b970-373f-48d8-8bf0-056b9bcf7d5e?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) reliant on any number of upstream goods are also at risk of losing access to crucial supplies, rendering their economic output, as well as the employment of their various workers, at increasing risk.
As bad as tariffs have already proven themselves for the global economy, however, they represent just the tip of the iceberg in terms of how Trump's governance is destroying the economy of the United States.
### **2\. Foreign tourism is collapsing**
Foreign tourism has long offered a shot in the arm of the U.S. economy. According to [the U.S. Department of Commerce](https://substack.com/redirect/b093c6e0-51ba-48b5-82c2-cfb337bf17b6?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), international tourists visiting the U.S. supported nearly 10 million jobs, spending over $225 billion in 2023.
In the years before Trump returned to office, 5.1% of the world's international travelers came to the U.S. Only two countries--France and Spain--drew more visitors. But even still, [the U.S. led the world in global tourism receipts](https://substack.com/redirect/df22948f-9cf4-44ee-b3b8-e37d6c43586b?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg): more than 1 out of every 4 dollars spent by international travelers in 2023 was spent within the U.S.
That influx of foreign money is already starting to wither. Motivated by stories of [travelers being detained](https://substack.com/redirect/cc941319-90d7-4309-a604-8544bfa2b9f0?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) and subjected to mistreatment for no apparent reason--or perhaps due to overt [ideological profiling](https://substack.com/redirect/523c5b7d-e45f-4b90-81b3-93fa6b106dc5?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) (about which I first raised a [public alarm](https://substack.com/redirect/3067c468-c036-49eb-9fe7-1f903649148e?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) in 2008)--international visitors have increasingly chosen other destinations.
The economic stimulus that they add to the economy has fallen through the floor over the past three months. Based on year-over-year data, foreign tourism last month [declined over 11%](https://substack.com/redirect/0a7fc8bf-40b0-4d3e-9902-0e112d9710c9?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) from the year before. Even if that trend grows no worse in the coming months, it will suck over $22 billion out of the U.S. economy this year.
That would be an optimistic outcome relative to the more likely possibility of continuing erosion.
Bookings from Canada (which sent [over 20 million visitors](https://substack.com/redirect/df22948f-9cf4-44ee-b3b8-e37d6c43586b?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) to the U.S. in 2023) have [dropped by over 70%](https://substack.com/redirect/405a4343-1a33-49b1-8c4d-5555f272a4d5?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) since the beginning of 2025. Well beyond Canada, other countries that have recently issued travel warnings to their citizens contemplating travel to the U.S. include Ireland, the Netherlands, Denmark, the United Kingdom, Germany, and Finland.
Countries including [Uruguay](https://substack.com/redirect/dc49d359-2884-4b36-9eeb-729d37941ede?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) and [Japan](https://substack.com/redirect/d4c1c0fe-651e-426b-970e-3b4908e011b7?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) had previously issued travel warnings to their citizens based on the [disturbing consistency of mass shootings](https://substack.com/redirect/3bfba655-002e-48e3-b986-3fc9e1cb21c8?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) in the U.S. But never have so many countries warned their citizens against traveling here.
The loss in America's [diplomatic standing](https://substack.com/redirect/1c7c9ea7-852b-4ff6-8e86-bd3b8b0641e4?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) in the world may seem lost on Americans who prefer to isolate from the international community. But the impact of travelers choosing other places to spend their--tens of billions of--vacation dollars will impose ripple effects throughout the American economy, particularly in [states that depend on tourism](https://substack.com/redirect/d1177634-7bd6-4f63-97ee-6b98944e88ae?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg).
### **3\. Intensifying labor shortages increasingly limit vulnerable industries**
Every day, new stories emerge about [ideological](https://substack.com/redirect/80fd15cc-f1b7-4b8e-a0e4-f3d7fa6fe80d?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), [racial](https://substack.com/redirect/ddc76058-5d04-4a75-8c8a-53108984ffc2?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), and [ethnic](https://substack.com/redirect/41309286-afe7-4948-a587-a7fcded241e0?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) profiling by federal immigration authorities. In addition, the previous [process](https://substack.com/redirect/f49186b9-2bf5-45a9-b541-7e926624e559?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) through which asylum seekers could seek long-term permanent residency has also been effectively shut down, closing one of the few legal pathways available to newcomers.
Each of these stories reflect an outrageous pattern of ignoring individual rights, as well as the broader rule of law. But they also indicate an economic problem that will continue to expand and cascade.
Across the United States, immigrants play disproportionate roles in several industries, including the service industry, construction, and agriculture.
For example, [in Florida, nearly 30% of jobs are held by immigrants](https://substack.com/redirect/b76d17aa-f7ea-4d08-b58d-2c3e481ccc73?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) (including both legal and undocumented migrants). Yet, despite its reliance on immigrant labor, Florida is has integrated local law enforcement with federal immigration enforcement to a greater degree than any other state.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/31340ddc-5697-4932-9257-1f8bd773cb23?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
Source: The Guardian US, available at https://www.theguardian.com/us-news/ng-interactive/2025/apr/30/trump-local-police-immigration
Community advocates across Florida warned in 2023, when the state adopted a law to strengthen immigration enforcement, that instilling fear among immigrant workers would threaten the state's economy.
Two years later, state [policymakers have introduced measures to legalize child labor](https://substack.com/redirect/80395a16-f960-4008-b5cf-c8b7d98c8b91?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), with no proposed restrictions that would prevent employers from scheduling employees as young as 14 years old for unlimited hours. The proposals mandate neither breaks, nor accommodations for school.
Florida may demonstrate the greatest economic vulnerability to deporting migrant laborers, but the rest of the country will also learn similar lessons.
[Share](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=1018597&post_id=162840926&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email&utm_content=share&utm_campaign=email-share&action=share&triggerShare=true&isFreemail=true&r=1hvr60&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTYyODQwOTI2LCJpYXQiOjE3NDY3NDI1ODcsImV4cCI6MTc0OTMzNDU4NywiaXNzIjoicHViLTEwMTg1OTciLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.4iCUSW4kX4Q2ByBx7YERlbhnLEODKskl_2gQGMbSxXA)
Around the country, immigrants account for roughly [30% of workers in the construction industry](https://substack.com/redirect/718691ef-bd30-46e7-9ff4-5360f90de7ae?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), and 40% of the industry's workers in California and Texas. Rather than occupy positions for which American citizens have been turned down, immigrant workers play a key role in providing human resources that the industry has found in short supply. In April 2024, the Associated General Contractors of America stated that "[labor availability has resumed being the number one challenge](https://substack.com/redirect/bd08481e-76ae-494f-b6a4-2b0c844c6b45?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) for many contractors."
Academics have confirmed that impression, and also explained why the pattern was thoroughly predictable. Yongwei Shan, assistant professor of Construction Engineering & Project Management at Oklahoma State University, explained that "fewer young people really want to work in the construction field because jobs are more physically demanding and construction job sites are more hazardous and exposed to weather. This might be [one] reason why you see [fewer U.S. citizens and more migrants in the construction fields](https://substack.com/redirect/bd08481e-76ae-494f-b6a4-2b0c844c6b45?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)."
Driving construction workers out of the country is a bad enough idea in the abstract. Doing so in the wake of various climate-related disasters devastating communities from [coastal California](https://substack.com/redirect/d4bab705-f98e-4508-8948-2ecdf9f3e8f1?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) to the [hills of Appalachia](https://substack.com/redirect/356bb0f5-6a1b-43c1-aed1-e971132fce0f?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) is especially harmful--not just to the ranks of immigrant laborers denied opportunities, and their families, but also the communities they serve that will continue to struggle to recruit labor for critical projects, from [municipal infrastructure](https://substack.com/redirect/356bb0f5-6a1b-43c1-aed1-e971132fce0f?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) to rebuilding [damaged and destroyed homes](https://substack.com/redirect/0a06b0f1-bf1f-49ff-a1bf-80511a62b14c?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg).
### **4\. Firing public workers has swelled the unemployment ranks**
Public spending in the U.S. has historically comprised as much as [45% of GDP](https://substack.com/redirect/e9d91732-e124-4e54-8375-7c7fbe3f01a2?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg). It would account for an even greater fraction if evaluated not only in direct expenditure, but also in the indirect spending by millions of public sector workers--from scientists to military service members, and from teachers to park & forest strangers--employed by the tens of thousands of [various local, state, and federal governments](https://substack.com/redirect/8cd7f0da-8ed8-4c7a-9f57-7d9bd3659d49?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) across the U.S.
While the Trump-Musk administration has framed its cuts to spending policies and [mass layoffs and firings](https://substack.com/redirect/44f2b390-505c-468f-a6f9-3d57f94b7251?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) as an effort to increase efficiency, nowhere does there appear any recognition of the profound impact of leaving hundreds of thousands of federal employees jobless.
[Over 60,000 federal workers were fired](https://substack.com/redirect/8660348f-5f90-44d1-adbd-e8b996d8fa2a?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) within the first two months of the new administration.
In direct terms, firing [tens of thousands of federal workers](https://substack.com/redirect/9fd19d75-640a-4c84-816e-5f791c251c48?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) removes from the economy all of the otherwise predictable spending by those employees, and their families, that would have supported the businesses and local economies where they live. Many of those workers have been [ordered by courts to be reinstated](https://substack.com/redirect/0999d27e-e788-43f9-bd8c-39ece9f24892?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), but it's [not clear](https://substack.com/redirect/266f8808-54b6-46f6-b330-3a0f046e75a1?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) whether the administration is willing to comply with court orders. Even those workers whose jobs do survive future legal appeals will probably spend much less on discretionary purchases until their employment appears more secure.
Every dollar that Trump & Musk claim to have "saved" through a layoff or firing is also an example of economic stimulus being removed from the consumer economy.
_This series will continue with an analysis of further ways that Trump administration policies are undermining America 's economy._
_In the meantime, paid subscribers can access a further section below that includes video of my debate with Trump 's counterterrorism czar, Sebastian Gorka. Gorka recently [proposed](https://substack.com/redirect/4140eb5c-03c9-45cd-af6a-1a9cc4d900c1?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) terrorism charges for political opponents, 14 years after I debated him on an international television news network about indefinite detention and civil liberties issues ignored at the time by Republicans and Democrats alike._
In 2011, I was working as Executive Director of the Bill of Rights Defense Committee (BORDC). BORDC, now known as [Defending Rights and Dissent](https://substack.com/redirect/ddf5689d-0047-45cb-a2d9-72195f5827f5?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) in the wake of a merger in 2015 with an allied organization, had been established by a transpartisan grassroots network to defend civil liberties in the wake of the 9-11 attacks ten years earlier.
As an advocate and organizer clamoring for Congress to respect civil liberties, I was grateful to receive an invitation from the team at Al-Jazeera that produces _Inside Story_. At the time, the issue inviting [debate](https://substack.com/redirect/9d75a779-9060-4664-b50a-c9d77fcfdd4e?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) was whether the U.S. government should be allowed to detain individuals--including U.S. citizens--without trial based on accusation alone.
Having raised concerns about civil liberties at a time when they were ignored by both Republicans and Democrats, I really wish more people had paid attention. The future--which we are now living today--could have been different.
[](https://substack.com/redirect/dbd565b3-7e50-47ca-8557-4974137f1dc1?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)

My greatest concerns center on his administration's erosion of rights, freedoms, and the rule of law--but its attacks on the economy seem to have drawn more widespread concern. This article observes a series of ways in which the Trump administration is not only , but also the American economy and its longstanding role in the global economy. In less than three months, these measures have threatened to unwind 75 years of post-war prosperity. | | ---|---|--- Ironically, the economic destruction invited by Trump's policies could do more good for the world than any amount of economic success possibly could. [Note: I 'll save that piece of the analysis for the final post in this continuing series. This introductory post concludes with another piece of bonus content, including video of me debating a senior Trump administration official a decade ago.] Bizarre expectations Among the greatest ironies of Trump's return to the White House is the seemingly inexplicable view-- by millions of seemingly oblivious voters--that his governance would somehow be good for the economy. While his brand has long been associated with garish opulence, does not seem to suggest acumen, so much as the fortune of being able to invest hundreds of millions of dollars in a series of more or less fraudulent projects. The destruction of the American economy may be the most grandiose among them. Thanks for reading Chronicles of a Dying Empire! You can help inform your friends by sharing this post. The stock market climbed in the days after Trump's inauguration to near-record peaks, but retreated in the time since then, losing its gains over the past year amidst a series of crashes and short-term recoveries that have no end in sight. While the impacts of that volatility in financial markets may most directly affect the investor class, many working Americans have long grown used to struggling to pay our bills and keep a roof over our heads. Our ranks are poised to swell immensely. 1\. Tariffs driving up costs and driving down business investment I began drafting this article well before revealed how deeply international capital is concerned about the recessionary impacts of tariffs. Americans have been widely relearning the lesson that too many seemed to have skipped in school: tariffs are paid not by the country on whom they are levied, but by . Among all of Trump's attacks on the global economy, tariffs have both because they have long been a central tenet of his vision, and also because they most directly affect other countries, and thereby the network of international capital that relies on (whatever passes for) "free" trade. The International Monetary Fund, for instance, has that the Trump tariffs could cost the global economy up to . Trump's tariffs threaten to across more or less every domestic and international manufacturing industry. Very few domestic industries produce parts entirely within the United States, making the relationship between tariffs and domestic manufacturing something like that of a wrench suddenly thrown into the wheel of a moving bicycle. Ironically, concerns about corporate globalization and the outsourcing of U.S. manufacturing capacity were ignored in the 90s--not only by Republicans, but by Democrats alike. Now that the outsourcing ship has sailed, the U.S. is particularly vulnerable to supply chain disruptions. That became clear during the COVID pandemic, when the U.S. suffered shortages of everything from personal to automobiles, . Chronicles of a Dying Empire is a publication supported by readers, rather than advertisers. To receive new posts,sign up for a free subscription! To support my work, please consider becoming a paid subscriber. American on imported goods (not to mention labor) suggests that the pain imposed by tariffs will only grow over time. The recent impacts of the Trump tariffs could easily pale in comparison to the eventual economic calamity that will emerge as product manufacturers, unable to secure the necessary supplies to deliver their products to the marketplace, eventually reduce their workforces. It's not just large businesses that are impacted by tariffs. reliant on any number of upstream goods are also at risk of losing access to crucial supplies, rendering their economic output, as well as the employment of their various workers, at increasing risk. As bad as tariffs have already proven themselves for the global economy, however, they represent just the tip of the iceberg in terms of how Trump's governance is destroying the economy of the United States. 2\. Foreign tourism is collapsing Foreign tourism has long offered a shot in the arm of the U.S. economy. According to , international tourists visiting the U.S. supported nearly 10 million jobs, spending over $225 billion in 2023. In the years before Trump returned to office, 5.1% of the world's international travelers came to the U.S. Only two countries--France and Spain--drew more visitors. But even still, : more than 1 out of every 4 dollars spent by international travelers in 2023 was spent within the U.S. That influx of foreign money is already starting to wither. Motivated by stories of and subjected to mistreatment for no apparent reason--or perhaps due to overt (about which I first raised a in 2008)--international visitors have increasingly chosen other destinations. Countries including and had previously issued travel warnings to their citizens based on the in the U.S. But never have so many countries warned their citizens against traveling here. The loss in America's in the world may seem lost on Americans who prefer to isolate from the international community. But the impact of travelers choosing other places to spend their--tens of billions of--vacation dollars will impose ripple effects throughout the American economy, particularly in . 3\. The candidate trusted by confused voters to improve the nation's economy is doing precisely what he said he would--and no one should be surprised by the results

# [A dozen ways that the Trump administration is destroying America's economy (Part I)](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=1018597&post_id=162840926&utm_source=post-email-title&utm_campaign=email-post-title&isFreemail=true&r=1hvr60&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTYyODQwOTI2LCJpYXQiOjE3NDYzOTM0NDEsImV4cCI6MTc0ODk4NTQ0MSwiaXNzIjoicHViLTEwMTg1OTciLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.4V02Rt7rpCiQ2e0sPZv_6WT3f3ddgDd7HF06P8QvcCY)
### The candidate trusted by confused voters to improve the nation's economy is doing precisely what he said he would--and no one should be surprised by the results
| | [Shahid Buttar](https://substack.com/@shahidbuttar)
---
| May 4| | | ∙| | Preview
---|---|---
| [](https://substack.com/@shahidbuttar)
---

---
| | | [](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=1018597&post_id=162840926&utm_source=substack&isFreemail=true&submitLike=true&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTYyODQwOTI2LCJyZWFjdGlvbiI6IuKdpCIsImlhdCI6MTc0NjM5MzQ0MSwiZXhwIjoxNzQ4OTg1NDQxLCJpc3MiOiJwdWItMTAxODU5NyIsInN1YiI6InJlYWN0aW9uIn0.g080d3uG46Ujzhnbb-lXsVf6O3TMm2RB78IWKPswRVU&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=email-reaction&r=1hvr60)

| | [](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=1018597&post_id=162840926&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email&isFreemail=true&comments=true&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTYyODQwOTI2LCJpYXQiOjE3NDYzOTM0NDEsImV4cCI6MTc0ODk4NTQ0MSwiaXNzIjoicHViLTEwMTg1OTciLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.4V02Rt7rpCiQ2e0sPZv_6WT3f3ddgDd7HF06P8QvcCY&r=1hvr60&utm_campaign=email-half-magic-comments&action=post-comment&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email)

| | [READ IN APP](https://open.substack.com/pub/shahidbuttar/p/a-dozen-ways-that-the-trump-administration?utm_source=email&redirect=app-store&utm_campaign=email-read-in-app)

Since returning to office barely three months ago, President Trump has waged a seemingly unrelenting "[blitzkrieg](https://substack.com/redirect/f70938d9-e898-4c36-bb93-0b8c1faf0a6c?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)" on America. My greatest concerns center on his administration's erosion of rights, freedoms, and the rule of law--but its attacks on the economy seem to have drawn more widespread concern.
This article observes a series of ways in which the Trump administration is [destroying](https://substack.com/redirect/5089d06d-7ee2-4d25-a27a-cda286c78542?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) not only [democracy](https://substack.com/redirect/81cd7503-7e71-4806-889f-ed44caf35967?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), but also the American economy and its longstanding role in the global economy. In less than three months, these measures have threatened to unwind 75 years of post-war prosperity.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/f6bcd666-9970-42c7-ac58-7892d90d1e4e?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
Ironically, the economic destruction invited by Trump's policies could do more good for the world than any amount of economic success possibly could. [_Note: I 'll save that piece of the analysis for the final post in this continuing series. This introductory post concludes with another piece of bonus content, including video of me debating a senior Trump administration official a decade ago._]
### B**izarre expectations**
Among the greatest ironies of Trump's return to the White House is the seemingly inexplicable view--[shared](https://substack.com/redirect/d297e34c-3397-47e2-a657-aa0cc8375833?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) by millions of seemingly oblivious voters--that his governance would somehow be good for the economy.
While his brand has long been associated with garish opulence, [Trump's history as a businessman](https://substack.com/redirect/3687dd10-00a5-438e-855b-450d47c5e4fa?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) does not seem to suggest acumen, so much as the fortune of being [born to a rich father](https://substack.com/redirect/f4601f3d-4f13-4824-9b38-fa93df025f85?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) able to invest hundreds of millions of dollars in a series of more or less fraudulent projects.
The destruction of the American economy may be the most grandiose among them.
_Thanks for reading Chronicles of a Dying Empire! You can help inform your friends by sharing this post._
[ Share](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=1018597&post_id=162840926&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email&utm_content=share&utm_campaign=email-share&action=share&triggerShare=true&isFreemail=true&r=1hvr60&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTYyODQwOTI2LCJpYXQiOjE3NDYzOTM0NDEsImV4cCI6MTc0ODk4NTQ0MSwiaXNzIjoicHViLTEwMTg1OTciLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.4V02Rt7rpCiQ2e0sPZv_6WT3f3ddgDd7HF06P8QvcCY)
The stock market climbed in the days after Trump's inauguration to near-record peaks, but retreated in the time since then, losing its gains over the past year amidst a series of crashes and short-term recoveries that have no end in sight. While the impacts of that volatility in financial markets may most directly affect the investor class, many working Americans have long grown used to struggling to pay our bills and keep a roof over our heads.
Our ranks are poised to swell immensely.
### **1\. Tariffs driving up costs and driving down business investment**
I began drafting this article well before [recent stock market shocks](https://substack.com/redirect/15840cfa-5267-4e0e-9b09-8ccf1b33dfc1?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) revealed how deeply international capital is concerned about the recessionary impacts of tariffs. Americans have been widely relearning the lesson that too many seemed to have skipped in school: tariffs are paid not by the country on whom they are levied, but by [domestic consumers of imported goods](https://substack.com/redirect/cc107f8e-059f-4281-83a0-0627c98885e0?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg).
Among all of Trump's attacks on the global economy, tariffs have [drawn the most attention](https://substack.com/redirect/32ff1dac-c835-4d9a-b9c2-97423feef431?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) both because they have long been a central tenet of his vision, and also because they most directly affect other countries, and thereby the network of international capital that relies on (whatever passes for) "free" trade. The International Monetary Fund, for instance, has [forecast](https://substack.com/redirect/19764663-c640-43a4-af88-7ba6cff7a3ec?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) that the Trump tariffs could cost the global economy up to [$1 trillion](https://substack.com/redirect/32ff1dac-c835-4d9a-b9c2-97423feef431?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg).
Trump's tariffs threaten to [upend the global supply chain](https://substack.com/redirect/66507cd2-6282-4c52-b746-2e7f05d43995?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) across more or less every domestic and international manufacturing industry. Very few domestic industries produce parts entirely within the United States, making the relationship between tariffs and domestic manufacturing something like that of a wrench suddenly thrown into the wheel of a moving bicycle.
Ironically, concerns about corporate globalization and the outsourcing of U.S. manufacturing capacity were ignored in the 90s--not only by Republicans, but by Democrats alike. Now that the outsourcing ship has sailed, the U.S. is particularly vulnerable to supply chain disruptions.
That became clear during the COVID pandemic, when the U.S. suffered shortages of everything from personal [protective equipment for hospital workers](https://substack.com/redirect/fbacdee1-a963-44c6-8dbd-f9ae7ae4a099?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) to automobiles, [computer chips and home appliances](https://substack.com/redirect/06bc579d-ac18-455b-b916-2ae41555cc56?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg).
_Chronicles of a Dying Empire is a publication supported by readers, rather than advertisers. To receive new posts,**sign up for a free subscription!** To support my work, please consider becoming a paid subscriber._
[Upgrade to paid](https://substack..7xnddIw0ufpQnQ83mJ3hdzG3An2qmNWtTjSTfmw7efc?&utm_medium=email&utm_source=subscribe-widget-preamble&utm_content=162840926)
American [reliance](https://substack.com/redirect/2c091f1d-3fb2-43ad-ba9f-2d553067930b?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) on imported goods (not to mention labor) suggests that the pain imposed by tariffs will only grow over time. The recent impacts of the Trump tariffs could easily pale in comparison to the eventual economic calamity that will emerge as product manufacturers, unable to secure the necessary supplies to deliver their products to the marketplace, eventually reduce their workforces.
It's not just large businesses that are impacted by tariffs. [Small businesses](https://substack.com/redirect/eba4b970-373f-48d8-8bf0-056b9bcf7d5e?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) reliant on any number of upstream goods are also at risk of losing access to crucial supplies, rendering their economic output, as well as the employment of their various workers, at increasing risk.
As bad as tariffs have already proven themselves for the global economy, however, they represent just the tip of the iceberg in terms of how Trump's governance is destroying the economy of the United States.
### **2\. Foreign tourism is collapsing**
Foreign tourism has long offered a shot in the arm of the U.S. economy. According to [the U.S. Department of Commerce](https://substack.com/redirect/b093c6e0-51ba-48b5-82c2-cfb337bf17b6?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), international tourists visiting the U.S. supported nearly 10 million jobs, spending over $225 billion in 2023.
In the years before Trump returned to office, 5.1% of the world's international travelers came to the U.S. Only two countries--France and Spain--drew more visitors. But even still, [the U.S. led the world in global tourism receipts](https://substack.com/redirect/df22948f-9cf4-44ee-b3b8-e37d6c43586b?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg): more than 1 out of every 4 dollars spent by international travelers in 2023 was spent within the U.S.
That influx of foreign money is already starting to wither. Motivated by stories of [travelers being detained](https://substack.com/redirect/cc941319-90d7-4309-a604-8544bfa2b9f0?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) and subjected to mistreatment for no apparent reason--or perhaps due to overt [ideological profiling](https://substack.com/redirect/523c5b7d-e45f-4b90-81b3-93fa6b106dc5?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) (about which I first raised a [public alarm](https://substack.com/redirect/3067c468-c036-49eb-9fe7-1f903649148e?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) in 2008)--international visitors have increasingly chosen other destinations.
The economic stimulus that they add to the economy has fallen through the floor over the past three months. Based on year-over-year data, foreign tourism last month [declined over 11%](https://substack.com/redirect/0a7fc8bf-40b0-4d3e-9902-0e112d9710c9?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) from the year before. Even if that trend grows no worse in the coming months, it will suck over $22 billion out of the U.S. economy this year.
That would be an optimistic outcome relative to the more likely possibility of continuing erosion.
Bookings from Canada (which sent [over 20 million visitors](https://substack.com/redirect/df22948f-9cf4-44ee-b3b8-e37d6c43586b?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) to the U.S. in 2023) have [dropped by over 70%](https://substack.com/redirect/405a4343-1a33-49b1-8c4d-5555f272a4d5?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) since the beginning of 2025. Well beyond Canada, other countries that have recently issued travel warnings to their citizens contemplating travel to the U.S. include Ireland, the Netherlands, Denmark, the United Kingdom, Germany, and Finland.
Countries including [Uruguay](https://substack.com/redirect/dc49d359-2884-4b36-9eeb-729d37941ede?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) and [Japan](https://substack.com/redirect/d4c1c0fe-651e-426b-970e-3b4908e011b7?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) had previously issued travel warnings to their citizens based on the [disturbing consistency of mass shootings](https://substack.com/redirect/3bfba655-002e-48e3-b986-3fc9e1cb21c8?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) in the U.S. But never have so many countries warned their citizens against traveling here.
The loss in America's [diplomatic standing](https://substack.com/redirect/1c7c9ea7-852b-4ff6-8e86-bd3b8b0641e4?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) in the world may seem lost on Americans who prefer to isolate from the international community. But the impact of travelers choosing other places to spend their--tens of billions of--vacation dollars will impose ripple effects throughout the American economy, particularly in [states that depend on tourism](https://substack.com/redirect/d1177634-7bd6-4f63-97ee-6b98944e88ae?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg).
### **3\. Intensifying labor shortages increasingly limit vulnerable industries**
Every day, new stories emerge about [ideological](https://substack.com/redirect/80fd15cc-f1b7-4b8e-a0e4-f3d7fa6fe80d?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), [racial](https://substack.com/redirect/ddc76058-5d04-4a75-8c8a-53108984ffc2?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), and [ethnic](https://substack.com/redirect/41309286-afe7-4948-a587-a7fcded241e0?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) profiling by federal immigration authorities. In addition, the previous [process](https://substack.com/redirect/f49186b9-2bf5-45a9-b541-7e926624e559?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) through which asylum seekers could seek long-term permanent residency has also been effectively shut down, closing one of the few legal pathways available to newcomers.
Each of these stories reflect an outrageous pattern of ignoring individual rights, as well as the broader rule of law. But they also indicate an economic problem that will continue to expand and cascade.
Across the United States, immigrants play disproportionate roles in several industries, including the service industry, construction, and agriculture.
For example, [in Florida, nearly 30% of jobs are held by immigrants](https://substack.com/redirect/b76d17aa-f7ea-4d08-b58d-2c3e481ccc73?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) (including both legal and undocumented migrants). Yet, despite its reliance on immigrant labor, Florida is has integrated local law enforcement with federal immigration enforcement to a greater degree than any other state.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/31340ddc-5697-4932-9257-1f8bd773cb23?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
Source: The Guardian US, available at https://www.theguardian.com/us-news/ng-interactive/2025/apr/30/trump-local-police-immigration
Community advocates across Florida warned in 2023, when the state adopted a law to strengthen immigration enforcement, that instilling fear among immigrant workers would threaten the state's economy.
Two years later, state [policymakers have introduced measures to legalize child labor](https://substack.com/redirect/80395a16-f960-4008-b5cf-c8b7d98c8b91?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), with no proposed restrictions that would prevent employers from scheduling employees as young as 14 years old for unlimited hours. The proposals mandate neither breaks, nor accommodations for school.
Florida may demonstrate the greatest economic vulnerability to deporting migrant laborers, but the rest of the country will also learn similar lessons.
[Share](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=1018597&post_id=162840926&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email&utm_content=share&utm_campaign=email-share&action=share&triggerShare=true&isFreemail=true&r=1hvr60&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTYyODQwOTI2LCJpYXQiOjE3NDYzOTM0NDEsImV4cCI6MTc0ODk4NTQ0MSwiaXNzIjoicHViLTEwMTg1OTciLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.4V02Rt7rpCiQ2e0sPZv_6WT3f3ddgDd7HF06P8QvcCY)
Around the country, immigrants account for roughly [30% of workers in the construction industry](https://substack.com/redirect/718691ef-bd30-46e7-9ff4-5360f90de7ae?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), and 40% of the industry's workers in California and Texas. Rather than occupy positions for which American citizens have been turned down, immigrant workers play a key role in providing human resources that the industry has found in short supply. In April 2024, the Associated General Contractors of America stated that "[labor availability has resumed being the number one challenge](https://substack.com/redirect/bd08481e-76ae-494f-b6a4-2b0c844c6b45?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) for many contractors."
Academics have confirmed that impression, and also explained why the pattern was thoroughly predictable. Yongwei Shan, assistant professor of Construction Engineering & Project Management at Oklahoma State University, explained that "fewer young people really want to work in the construction field because jobs are more physically demanding and construction job sites are more hazardous and exposed to weather. This might be [one] reason why you see [fewer U.S. citizens and more migrants in the construction fields](https://substack.com/redirect/bd08481e-76ae-494f-b6a4-2b0c844c6b45?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)."
Driving construction workers out of the country is a bad enough idea in the abstract. Doing so in the wake of various climate-related disasters devastating communities from [coastal California](https://substack.com/redirect/d4bab705-f98e-4508-8948-2ecdf9f3e8f1?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) to the [hills of Appalachia](https://substack.com/redirect/356bb0f5-6a1b-43c1-aed1-e971132fce0f?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) is especially harmful--not just to the ranks of immigrant laborers denied opportunities, and their families, but also the communities they serve that will continue to struggle to recruit labor for critical projects, from [municipal infrastructure](https://substack.com/redirect/356bb0f5-6a1b-43c1-aed1-e971132fce0f?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) to rebuilding [damaged and destroyed homes](https://substack.com/redirect/0a06b0f1-bf1f-49ff-a1bf-80511a62b14c?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg).
### **4\. Firing public workers has swelled the unemployment ranks**
Public spending in the U.S. has historically comprised as much as [45% of GDP](https://substack.com/redirect/e9d91732-e124-4e54-8375-7c7fbe3f01a2?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg). It would account for an even greater fraction if evaluated not only in direct expenditure, but also in the indirect spending by millions of public sector workers--from scientists to military service members, and from teachers to park & forest strangers--employed by the tens of thousands of [various local, state, and federal governments](https://substack.com/redirect/8cd7f0da-8ed8-4c7a-9f57-7d9bd3659d49?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) across the U.S.
While the Trump-Musk administration has framed its cuts to spending policies and [mass layoffs and firings](https://substack.com/redirect/44f2b390-505c-468f-a6f9-3d57f94b7251?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) as an effort to increase efficiency, nowhere does there appear any recognition of the profound impact of leaving hundreds of thousands of federal employees jobless.
[Over 60,000 federal workers were fired](https://substack.com/redirect/8660348f-5f90-44d1-adbd-e8b996d8fa2a?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) within the first two months of the new administration.
In direct terms, firing [tens of thousands of federal workers](https://substack.com/redirect/9fd19d75-640a-4c84-816e-5f791c251c48?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) removes from the economy all of the otherwise predictable spending by those employees, and their families, that would have supported the businesses and local economies where they live. Many of those workers have been [ordered by courts to be reinstated](https://substack.com/redirect/0999d27e-e788-43f9-bd8c-39ece9f24892?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), but it's [not clear](https://substack.com/redirect/266f8808-54b6-46f6-b330-3a0f046e75a1?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) whether the administration is willing to comply with court orders. Even those workers whose jobs do survive future legal appeals will probably spend much less on discretionary purchases until their employment appears more secure.
Every dollar that Trump & Musk claim to have "saved" through a layoff or firing is also an example of economic stimulus being removed from the consumer economy.
_This series will continue with an analysis of further ways that Trump administration policies are undermining America 's economy._
_In the meantime, paid subscribers can access a further section below that includes video of my debate with Trump 's counterterrorism czar, Sebastian Gorka. Gorka recently [proposed](https://substack.com/redirect/4140eb5c-03c9-45cd-af6a-1a9cc4d900c1?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) terrorism charges for political opponents, 14 years after I debated him on an international television news network about indefinite detention and civil liberties issues ignored at the time by Republicans and Democrats alike..._

The candidate trusted by confused voters to improve the nation's economy is doing precisely what he said he would--and no one should be surprised by the results | | | May 4| | | ∙| | Preview ---|---|--- | | | | | | | | | | | | Since returning to office barely three months ago, President Trump has waged a seemingly unrelenting "" on America. My greatest concerns center on his administration's erosion of rights, freedoms, and the rule of law--but its attacks on the economy seem to have drawn more widespread concern. This article observes a series of ways in which the Trump administration is not only , but also the American economy and its longstanding role in the global economy. In less than three months, these measures have threatened to unwind 75 years of post-war prosperity. | | ---|---|--- Ironically, the economic destruction invited by Trump's policies could do more good for the world than any amount of economic success possibly could. [Note: I 'll save that piece of the analysis for the final post in this continuing series. This introductory post concludes with another piece of bonus content, including video of me debating a senior Trump administration official a decade ago.] Bizarre expectations Among the greatest ironies of Trump's return to the White House is the seemingly inexplicable view-- by millions of seemingly oblivious voters--that his governance would somehow be good for the economy. While his brand has long been associated with garish opulence, does not seem to suggest acumen, so much as the fortune of being able to invest hundreds of millions of dollars in a series of more or less fraudulent projects. The destruction of the American economy may be the most grandiose among them. Thanks for reading Chronicles of a Dying Empire! You can help inform your friends by sharing this post. The stock market climbed in the days after Trump's inauguration to near-record peaks, but retreated in the time since then, losing its gains over the past year amidst a series of crashes and short-term recoveries that have no end in sight. While the impacts of that volatility in financial markets may most directly affect the investor class, many working Americans have long grown used to struggling to pay our bills and keep a roof over our heads. Our ranks are poised to swell immensely. 1\. Tariffs driving up costs and driving down business investment I began drafting this article well before revealed how deeply international capital is concerned about the recessionary impacts of tariffs. Americans have been widely relearning the lesson that too many seemed to have skipped in school: tariffs are paid not by the country on whom they are levied, but by . Among all of Trump's attacks on the global economy, tariffs have both because they have long been a central tenet of his vision, and also because they most directly affect other countries, and thereby the network of international capital that relies on (whatever passes for) "free" trade. The International Monetary Fund, for instance, has that the Trump tariffs could cost the global economy up to . Trump's tariffs threaten to across more or less every domestic and international manufacturing industry. Very few domestic industries produce parts entirely within the United States, making the relationship between tariffs and domestic manufacturing something like that of a wrench suddenly thrown into the wheel of a moving bicycle. Ironically, concerns about corporate globalization and the outsourcing of U.S. manufacturing capacity were ignored in the 90s--not only by Republicans, but by Democrats alike. Now that the outsourcing ship has sailed, the U.S. is particularly vulnerable to supply chain disruptions. That became clear during the COVID pandemic, when the U.S. suffered shortages of everything from personal to automobiles, . Chronicles of a Dying Empire is a publication supported by readers, rather than advertisers. To receive new posts,sign up for a free subscription! To support my work, please consider becoming a paid subscriber. American on imported goods (not to mention labor) suggests that the pain imposed by tariffs will only grow over time. The recent impacts of the Trump tariffs could easily pale in comparison to the eventual economic calamity that will emerge as product manufacturers, unable to secure the necessary supplies to deliver their products to the marketplace, eventually reduce their workforces. It's not just large businesses that are impacted by tariffs. reliant on any number of upstream goods are also at risk of losing access to crucial supplies, rendering their economic output, as well as the employment of their various workers, at increasing risk. As bad as tariffs have already proven themselves for the global economy, however, they represent just the tip of the iceberg in terms of how Trump's governance is destroying the economy of the United States. 2\. Foreign tourism is collapsing Foreign tourism has long offered a shot in the arm of the U.S. economy. According to , international tourists visiting the U.S. supported nearly 10 million jobs, spending over $225 billion in 2023. In the years before Trump returned to office, 5.1% of the world's international travelers came to the U.S. Only two countries--France and Spain--drew more visitors. But even still, : more than 1 out of every 4 dollars spent by international travelers in 2023 was spent within the U.S. That influx of foreign money is already starting to wither. Motivated by stories of and subjected to mistreatment for no apparent reason--or perhaps due to overt (about which I first raised a in 2008)--international visitors have increasingly chosen other destinations. The economic stimulus that they add to the economy has fallen through the floor over the past three months. Based on year-over-year data, foreign tourism last month from the year before. Even if that trend grows no worse in the coming months, it will suck over $22 billion out of the U.S. economy this year. That would be an optimistic outcome relative to the more likely possibility of continuing erosion. Bookings from Canada (which sent to the U.S. in 2023) have since the beginning of 2025. Well beyond Canada, other countries that have recently issued travel warnings to their citizens contemplating travel to the U.S. include Ireland, the Netherlands, Denmark, the United Kingdom, Germany, and Finland. Countries including and had previously issued travel warnings to their citizens based on the in the U.S. But never have so many countries warned their citizens against traveling here. The loss in America's in the world may seem lost on Americans who prefer to isolate from the international community. But the impact of travelers choosing other places to spend their--tens of billions of--vacation dollars will impose ripple effects throughout the American economy, particularly in . 3\. Intensifying labor shortages increasingly limit vulnerable industries Every day, new stories emerge about , , and profiling by federal immigration authorities. In addition, the previous through which asylum seekers could seek long-term permanent residency has also been effectively shut down, closing one of the few legal pathways available to newcomers. Each of these stories reflect an outrageous pattern of ignoring individual rights, as well as the broader rule of law. But they also indicate an economic problem that will continue to expand and cascade. Across the United States, immigrants play disproportionate roles in several industries, including the service industry, construction, and agriculture. For example, (including both legal and undocumented migrants). Yet, despite its reliance on immigrant labor, Florida is has integrated local law enforcement with federal immigration enforcement to a greater degree than any other state. | | ---|---|--- Source: The Guardian US, available at Community advocates across Florida warned in 2023, when the state adopted a law to strengthen immigration enforcement, that instilling fear among immigrant workers would threaten the state's economy. Two years later, state , with no proposed restrictions that would prevent employers from scheduling employees as young as 14 years old for unlimited hours. The proposals mandate neither breaks, nor accommodations for school. Florida may demonstrate the greatest economic vulnerability to deporting migrant laborers, but the rest of the country will also learn similar lessons. Around the country, immigrants account for roughly , and 40% of the industry's workers in California and Texas. Rather than occupy positions for which American citizens have been turned down, immigrant workers play a key role in providing human resources that the industry has found in short supply. In April 2024, the Associated General Contractors of America stated that " for many contractors." Academics have confirmed that impression, and also explained why the pattern was thoroughly predictable. Yongwei Shan, assistant professor of Construction Engineering & Project Management at Oklahoma State University, explained that "fewer young people really want to work in the construction field because jobs are more physically demanding and construction job sites are more hazardous and exposed to weather. This might be [one] reason why you see ." Driving construction workers out of the country is a bad enough idea in the abstract. Doing so in the wake of various climate-related disasters devastating communities from to the is especially harmful--not just to the ranks of immigrant laborers denied opportunities, and their families, but also the communities they serve that will continue to struggle to recruit labor for critical projects, from to rebuilding . 4\. Firing public workers has swelled the unemployment ranks Public spending in the U.S. has historically comprised as much as . It would account for an even greater fraction if evaluated not only in direct expenditure, but also in the indirect spending by millions of public sector workers--from scientists to military service members, and from teachers to park & forest strangers--employed by the tens of thousands of across the U.S. While the Trump-Musk administration has framed its cuts to spending policies and as an effort to increase efficiency, nowhere does there appear any recognition of the profound impact of leaving hundreds of thousands of federal employees jobless. within the first two months of the new administration. In direct terms, firing removes from the economy all of the otherwise predictable spending by those employees, and their families, that would have supported the businesses and local economies where they live. Many of those workers have been , but it's whether the administration is willing to comply with court orders. Even those workers whose jobs do survive future legal appeals will probably spend much less on discretionary purchases until their employment appears more secure. Every dollar that Trump & Musk claim to have "saved" through a layoff or firing is also an example of economic stimulus being removed from the consumer economy. This series will continue with an analysis of further ways that Trump administration policies are undermining America 's economy. In the meantime, paid subscribers can access a further section below that includes video of my debate with Trump 's counterterrorism czar, Sebastian Gorka. Gorka recently terrorism charges for political opponents, 14 years after I debated him on an international television news network about indefinite detention and civil liberties issues ignored at the time by Republicans and Democrats alike... | Using _“The Wheel of Privilege_ ," Manchester is teaching students that they are either marginalized or powerful based on the color of their skin, their sex, age, familial status and income level among other factors. There are even slideshows about how “Whiteness is Power” and how mispronouncing a person's name might mean **_you_** are racist. It's time that the teachers' unions and school administrators focus on educating our children - not indoctrinating them with far left political ideals. NH Senate Republicans have been proud to champion transparency in our local school districts **_and_** Education Freedom Accounts. We need to make sure that schools are **_free from the Democrats’ DEI policies._**
When it comes to protecting our kids in the classroom, every NH Senate Republican has your back, can we count on your support? ### "My daughter changed her identity, not after years of discussion or family dialogue, no, this happened quickly and secretly..."
May 4
---

---
| | | [](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=3072780&post_id=162843477&utm_source=substack&isFreemail=true&submitLike=true&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTYyODQzNDc3LCJyZWFjdGlvbiI6IuKdpCIsImlhdCI6MTc0NjM5NzIxOCwiZXhwIjoxNzQ4OTg5MjE4LCJpc3MiOiJwdWItMzA3Mjc4MCIsInN1YiI6InJlYWN0aW9uIn0.FAjoaFBwCCcewLP6pq-LhdE2GN9IGAbwASE-of0HW6Y&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=email-reaction&r=1hvr60)

| | [READ IN APP](https://open.substack.com/pub/politibrawl/p/furious-father-confronts-school-board?utm_source=email&redirect=app-store&utm_campaign=email-read-in-app)
---

A furious Colorado father, Dustin Gonzalez, **[went off at woke educators](https://substack.com/redirect/0b3b2d44-2235-4fd4-a53e-2b39775fc931?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)** during a county school board meeting, saying his daughter's new gender identity and transition was kept secret from him and affirmed by school staff.
[Watch Video](https://substack.com/redirect/0b3b2d44-2235-4fd4-a53e-2b39775fc931?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
[](https://substack.com/redirect/0b3b2d44-2235-4fd4-a53e-2b39775fc931?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
"I'm fighting to make sure what happened to me never happens to another parent!" Gonzalez told the board. "My daughter changed her identity, not after years of discussion or family dialogue, not after joint input from both of her parents, no, this happened quickly, secretly, encouraged by school-appointed therapists in a system that never though to include me!"
"By the time I found out, I was already labeled a problem!" Gonzalez continued. "Your systems made it possible, and your silence made it personal. You never once thought 'Shouldn't the father be involved, too.'"
Gonzalez explained that he faces the risk of losing parenting time with his daughter, simply for not affirming her new identity. He further claimed that the loss of visitation rights was pushed by his ex wife who supports and affirms the gender transition of their daughter.
"You gave my ex all the tools she needed to take our daughter from me!"
**[Watch the shocking confrontation here:](https://substack.com/redirect/0b3b2d44-2235-4fd4-a53e-2b39775fc931?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)**
[ Watch Video](https://substack.com/redirect/0b3b2d44-2235-4fd4-a53e-2b39775fc931?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
| | POLL
---
What would you do if you found out your child transitioned secretly at school?

# Trump gives a serious answer about running in 2028
President Donald Trump gave NBC News' Kristen Welker **[the real answer](https://substack.com/redirect/976cc6cf-68e9-43a1-b263-164ef6cd4ff1?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)** to the question of if he will run for a third term in 2028.
[](https://substack.com/redirect/3eec81d8-f8e3-4c4d-b431-8370840ccb77?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
"It's something that, to the best of my knowledge, you're not allowed to do. There are many people selling the 2028 hat. But this is not something I'm looking to do. I'm looking to have four great years and turn it over to somebody."
Trump has previously joked about running for a third term, and left his real intentions on the matter ambiguous. His son, Eric Trump, posted a photo to social media of himself wearing a "Trump 2028" hat clearly in an effort to troll those with Trump Derangement Syndrome.
* * *
## _PolitiBrawl Opinion Piece_
# Why cable news died after Tucker Carlson left Fox News
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/1d0e7f6a-bf18-4ecf-a605-d70bc8bf86c3?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
When I first met Tucker Carlson in the summer of 2017, I sat down in front of the broad, sturdy wooden desk in his Fox News office expecting to discuss my role in spearheading the Caller's new video operation.

I introduced myself and Tucker replied, "Richie McGinniss? That sounds like a famous person's name!" One thing even Tucker's adversaries can't deny after meeting him, he is a charming guy.
I had just started my job at The Daily Caller, a company that he founded in 2010, while Tucker had just taken over the prime-time 8 PM slot vacated by Bill O'Reilly after some salacious revelations.
Despite the notoriety of his new gig, Tucker was more interested in hearing about where I came from and what made me tick.
I had just lost my father and, as an actual family man rather than pretending to be one on TV, Tucker wanted to know more about how my family was doing after the loss, and who my dad was. I mentioned that, though he was a doctor, my dad and I watched the news together every night growing up, and my dad's predictions of what would come of the Iraq War were spot on. I also told him that we often made fun of the robotic voices of the correspondents, whose on-camera personae were a far cry from their personalities in real life...
[Read Full Piece](https://substack.com/redirect/1d0e7f6a-bf18-4ecf-a605-d70bc8bf86c3?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)

name: DC Daily Journal
subject: Thomas Sowell: Trickle Down Theory and Tax Cuts for the Rich
body:
Please read the following message from our sponsor.
* * *

| | | | [](https://click.em.dcdailyjournal.com/t/c/ccb026964a3645458bd74a81ce9b15d5-73638988a6eb413fba50a1f8d4cde5b5/63)
---
| | Dear Friend, I hear from people all the time that Thomas Sowell is one of their heroes. For those people—and you may be among them—I’m thrilled to share a digital copy of one of his commentaries with you, absolutely free: _[**_“_**** _Trickle Down” Theory and “Tax Cuts for the Rich._****_”_**](https://click.em.dcdailyjournal.com/t/c/ccb026964a3645458bd74a81ce9b15d5-73638988a6eb413fba50a1f8d4cde5b5/63)_
---
| [](https://click.em.dcdailyjournal.com/t/c/ccb026964a3645458bd74a81ce9b15d5-73638988a6eb413fba50a1f8d4cde5b5/63)
---
| | “Tax cuts for the rich” is a favorite argument of those who oppose lower tax rates. But does imposing higher tax rates on wealthy Americans actually increase the amount of revenue coming into the federal government? Hoover Institution Senior Fellow **Thomas Sowell takes an empirical approach** in his commentary, [** _“_**** _Trickle Down” Theory and “Tax Cuts for the Rich._****_”_**](https://click.em.dcdailyjournal.com/t/c/ccb026964a3645458bd74a81ce9b15d5-73638988a6eb413fba50a1f8d4cde5b5/63) Sowell separates fact from fiction when it comes to tax rates and uses real life data and examples to prove that lower tax rates for all actually lead to greater revenue for the government. As a friend and supporter of the Hoover Institution, we are proud to offer you this valuable, thought-provoking resource free of charge. [**Click here to get your free copy of**** _“Trickle Down” Theory and “Tax Cuts for the Rich”_****now.**](https://click.em.dcdailyjournal.com/t/c/ccb026964a3645458bd74a81ce9b15d5-73638988a6eb413fba50a1f8d4cde5b5/63) Thanks, Greg Greg Stamps | _Online Development_ Hoover Institution | Stanford University
---
_With its eminent scholars and world-renowned Library and Archives, the Hoover Institution is a non-partisan, data-driven policy research organization that seeks to improve the human condition by advancing ideas that promote economic prosperity, national security, and democratic governance. Over more than one hundred years, Hoover’s work has directly led to policies that have produced greater freedom, democracy, and opportunity in the United States and around the world._
---

name: Deb Haaland
subject: Trump says he’s “bringing Columbus Day back” 🙄
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR AND AUTHORIZED BY DEB FOR NEW MEXICO
body:
Trump may be lost, but I know exactly where I am.
# [](https://act.debhaaland.com/go/133?t=1&akid=1742%2E400544%2EEJYfTn)
A few days ago Donald Trump proclaimed that he was “bringing Columbus Day back from the ashes.”
**I have a message for Donald Trump: Columbus was lost, and so are you.**
[](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em_ms_dh_20250504_1_columbus?refcode=em20250504-1742&refcode2=1742_400544_EJYfTn&akid=1742%2E400544%2EEJYfTn)
As Trump and his billionaire buddies make the economy worse for working families, slash federal programs and services that New Mexicans rely on, and further rig the system in their own favor, he’s hoping people will be distracted by his taunts and trolling.
**Trump may be lost, but I know exactly where I am, who I come from, and what we need to do to make the system work better for everyday families across New Mexico.**
But I’m running a grassroots campaign, and I’m counting on your support to keep it up. With the economy so uncertain, I know not everyone has spare money to donate, and that’s okay! We can still get this done if each of us does what we can.
* * *
**→** **For high earners:** If you’re in a position to give more, your contribution could go a long way toward funding this campaign and keeping the governor’s office blue. [**Please consider pitching in $1,000, $500, $250, $100, or as much as you can.**](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em_ms_dh_20250504_1_columbus?refcode=em20250504-1742&refcode2=1742_400544_EJYfTn&akid=1742%2E400544%2EEJYfTn)
**→** **For medium earners:** Any amount you can pitch in is truly appreciated, and when your grassroots donation is combined with thousands of others, everything adds up to make a powerful difference. [**Pitch in $25, $10, or whatever you’re able.**](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em_ms_dh_20250504_1_columbus?refcode=em20250504-1742&refcode2=1742_400544_EJYfTn&akid=1742%2E400544%2EEJYfTn)
**→** **If you can’t donate:** Like I said, that’s ok! You are an important member of our movement, and you can still make a big difference in this race by spreading the word on social media and letting your friends and family know about our campaign.
* * *
**Whatever you can do, I can’t thank you enough for your support.**
Together we’ll keep fighting to protect New Mexico from this administration’s harmful policies, and we’ll continue celebrating Indigenous heritage at the same time — no matter what Trump has to say about it.
In solidarity,
Deb Haaland
**[Donate »](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em_ms_dh_footer?refcode=em20250504-1&t=2&refcode2=1742_400544_EJYfTn&akid=1742%2E400544%2EEJYfTn)**
* * *
This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Email is a critical way we communicate with grassroots supporters like you. However, if you would like to change your subscription preferences, here are some options:
* [Receive Fewer Emails](https://act.debhaaland.com/signup/receive-fewer-emails-dh/?t=3&akid=1742%2E400544%2EEJYfTn)
* [Update Your Information](https://act.debhaaland.com/signup/update-your-information-dh/)
* [Unsubscribe](https://act.debhaaland.com/unsubscribe/unsubscribe-dh/?t=4&akid=1742%2E400544%2EEJYfTn)

Email us: [info@debhaaland.com](mailto:info@debhaaland.com)
PAID FOR AND AUTHORIZED BY DEB FOR NEW MEXICO

subject: The counter to drag queen story hour
body:
See you at the library| | | [](http://links.freedomfirstalert.com/u/click?_t=9ca143e971904d26a973d002cb0ae7f7&_m=da390802ed324ca88d11850a404ed6c7&_e=NLeTHtgOVriwQh1Jscm8gl0P5TkNyJb16Z1RasxpcK_fA-ROo2oWGJFQ788GDECXtqboX0wBF5cn_dc2QPT2YG0-f70Pi4ggtMYtI-v3tWMfQGtcUa5T41rYlqc0fOyji_1gUVpfYY081SKOBZdu78TueJzMPYFPYUizgkre4BGi0ZvRnyXa_Jk491rCMrya8D4iArh83L6TFYC7HZI7HCzZy0bwqMW5UAJFEQKJu-ShBEwYHqtuhoWjyEv3489bwQFhWwuXv-1SluVDUHE4ayM2vjtNb6mZLKlIZNyrIApaFQFUAtDaLT2n6ClEeJiQo87YjD7X5PJQQ6F-WnBSfsRjCWesGJHobhUMSF5beO31qWzEimSvP1HjiHZJl4knSkfVqNIYNXOpfynKNUVqOA%3D%3D)
---
| | Fellow Conservative,Before you do anything else today, will you please take a moment to **[sponsor a gift for our “See You at the Library Day” in the next 48 hours?](http://links.freedomfirstalert.com/u/click?_t=9ca143e971904d26a973d002cb0ae7f7&_m=da390802ed324ca88d11850a404ed6c7&_e=NLeTHtgOVriwQh1Jscm8gl0P5TkNyJb16Z1RasxpcK_fA-ROo2oWGJFQ788GDECXtqboX0wBF5cn_dc2QPT2YG0-f70Pi4ggtMYtI-v3tWMfQGtcUa5T41rYlqc0fOyji_1gUVpfYY081SKOBZdu78TueJzMPYFPYUizgkre4BGi0ZvRnyXa_Jk491rCMrya8D4iArh83L6TFYC7HZI7HCzZy0bwqMW5UAJFEQKJu-ShBEwYHqtuhoWjyEv3489bTbHj3GmwtkEMqLT39mVU7zTTs7dpN6Ro7uvkwxMA-Ucb7QIoltkao2c_N0EjymK7l1yviDiNY8Eaev5s6XhqFuUvflsY_oFipz9wBT71Gq7tfnpdZE5t1EBpYNHTnUUbv4rf7btIpTvOsoUxIepLkA%3D%3D)** If you haven’t heard about the “See You at the Library Day” on Fox News or Newsmax or your other favorite news sources, please let me tell you why it’s so critical.
---
Back when you were growing up, you may remember lazy Saturdays at the library with a kind but stern librarian. She would fill your mind with classics like _Pat the Bunny_ and _The Little Engine That Could_. And eventually you got old enough to roam by yourself and discover adventures like the _Three Musketeers_ , _Treasure Island_ and _Charlotte’s Web_.But today, all that has changed.The president of the American Library Association (ALA) is a self-described “Marxist lesbian.”Radical librarians call _Adventure of Huckleberry Finn_ and _To Kill a Mockingbird_ “racist.” They’ve stripped Laura Ingalls Wilder’s name from a prestigious book award because of her “problematic” _Little House_ series.And they bring men in wearing full makeup, dresses, and high heels for “Drag Queen Story Hours” where books like these are read to children of all ages... A is for Activist. An ABC board book for “families who want their kids to grow up in a space that is unapologetic about activism, environmental justice, civil rights, LGBTQ rights, and everything else that activists believe in and fight for...”

| Woke Baby. “For all the littlest progressives, waking up to seize a new day of justice and activism. Woke babies raise their fists in the air. Woke babies cry out for justice. Woke babies grow up to change the world.”
